title: What I learned from a three-day Postgres migration that should have taken four hours
subtitle: I trusted the ORM's query planner too early and paid for it in production
metaDescription: Postmortem on a Postgres migration that encountered unexpected query planner issues.
bodyHtml: I have run migrations like this a dozen times. This one decided it was different. Here is what I got wrong and why I will not get it wrong the same way again.

title: Replacing my shell aliases with a 200-line Go binary
subtitle: I wanted autocomplete and ended up with something I actually maintain
metaDescription: How I replaced a growing .zshrc aliases section with a small Go CLI tool.
bodyHtml: My .zshrc had grown to 340 lines. About 200 of those were aliases I had added across five years and never reviewed. I decided the problem was the format, not the content.

title: Notes on debugging a memory leak I introduced three months ago
subtitle: The fix was one line. Finding it took two full days and a lot of pprof output
metaDescription: A debugging diary for a Go memory leak introduced via an accidental closure capture.
bodyHtml: The allocation graph looked wrong in February. I noted it, assumed it was a profiling artifact, and moved on. By May it was unmistakable.

title: Six months with a mechanical keyboard I was sure I would hate
subtitle: I was wrong about the noise, right about the weight, and surprised by the rest
metaDescription: Six-month review of a mechanical keyboard from a former membrane keyboard user.
bodyHtml: I bought the keyboard to settle an argument with myself. I had been skeptical for years and thought I should at least give the thing a fair trial before continuing to dismiss it.

title: Building a static site generator over a long weekend: what I would do differently
subtitle: It works. I do not recommend building your own. Here is why I did it anyway
metaDescription: Retrospective on building a static site generator from scratch over a weekend.
bodyHtml: Every developer eventually considers writing their own static site generator. Most come to their senses. I did not, and I have some notes about that.

title: How I use SQLite for projects where most people reach for Redis
subtitle: Fewer moving parts, predictable performance, and no ops overhead
metaDescription: Using SQLite as a simple cache and queue instead of Redis for small projects.
bodyHtml: This is not a take-down of Redis. Redis is excellent. But for a certain class of project, the operational overhead is a tax on work that does not need it.

title: Tooling note: why I switched back to vim after two years with VS Code
subtitle: It was not about the modal editing. It was about what I stopped thinking about
metaDescription: Tooling note on returning to vim after a period using VS Code.
bodyHtml: VS Code made me productive faster than anything I had used before. It also made me dependent on a system I did not understand. That bothered me more over time, not less.

title: On shipping software you are not proud of
subtitle: Sometimes the codebase is bad and the business is fine and both things are true at once
metaDescription: Reflection on shipping imperfect software and what that means professionally.
bodyHtml: I have shipped code that I would not show anyone. Code that works, that users depend on, that makes money, and that I find embarrassing to read. This seems to be a normal part of the job.
